title: batch-bug-shepherd subagent return shapes
description: Two BBS-owned return shapes (triage / strategic-alignment). Driver and conflict-resolution returns validate against shepherd-driver assets/completion-schema.json (loaded by the orchestrator as ../shepherd-driver/assets/completion-schema.json), not this schema. Schema-validate every subagent return. On malformed, re-spawn ONCE; on second malformed, mark the row UNCLEAR (triage) or route as aligned with gate-failed-open annotation (strategic-alignment) and continue.

repro_steps.description: Smallest possible reproduction; required when verdict=LEGIT.

evidence.description: Command output, file paths, or commit refs that justify the verdict.

cited_principle.description: Verbatim section heading OR short sentence from PRINCIPLES.md that fires (for demote verdicts) or grounds the aligned verdict. On gate-fail-open infrastructure failures, the literal string 'gate-failed-open: <reason>'. NEVER empty; the gate fails OPEN to 'aligned' if no principle is citable, rather than demoting under infrastructure failure (truth #3 OUTPUT IS PROBABILISTIC + truth #4 HALLUCINATION IS INHERENT).

rationale.description: 1-3 sentences explaining the verdict in CEO voice. References the cited principle by name.

reservations.description: Required when verdict=aligned-with-reservations. Each entry is one specific concern downstream phases (panel / completion) must surface in their review prose. Omitted for other verdicts.
